pattern: Maintaining both value-to-count and count-to-how-many-values maps lets each prefix be tested in O(1) for whether deleting one element equalizes all frequencies.
hints: Walk the prefixes left to right while tracking, for every value, how many times it has appeared, and for every frequency, how many distinct values have that frequency.
A prefix qualifies only in a few shapes: every value appears once; one value appears once and the rest share a count; or all but one value share count c and a single value has count c+1.
Keep a frequency-of-frequencies table so each of those shape checks is constant time, and remember the largest qualifying prefix length.

edge_inputs.description: removing one breaks a near-uniform prefix
a lone trailing value after balanced triples
two equal values form a removable pair
one value has exactly one extra over the others
all the same value so removing one keeps it uniform
all distinct so any single removal leaves all frequencies one

stress.description: random small-alphabet array so collisions and varied frequencies occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    hi = max(2, n // 3)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, hi) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: small value range forces repeated counts that stress the frequency logic
